root_text.sn16.7:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe…
root_text.sn16.7:1.3: ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ mahākassapaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn16.7:1.6: Ahaṁ vā, kassapa, bhikkhū ovadeyyaṁ tvaṁ vā;
root_text.sn16.7:1.7: ahaṁ vā bhikkhūnaṁ dhammiṁ kathaṁ kareyyaṁ tvaṁ vā”ti.
root_text.sn16.7:2.1: “Dubbacā kho, bhante, etarahi bhikkhū, dovacassakaraṇehi dhammehi samannāgatā akkhamā appadakkhiṇaggāhino anusāsaniṁ.
root_text.sn16.7:2.2: Yassa kassaci, bhante, saddhā natthi kusalesu dhammesu, hirī natthi kusalesu dhammesu, ottappaṁ natthi kusalesu dhammesu, vīriyaṁ natthi kusalesu dhammesu, paññā natthi kusalesu dhammesu, tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, hāniyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu, no vuddhi.
root_text.sn16.7:3.1: Seyyathāpi, bhante, kāḷapakkhe candassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, hāyateva vaṇṇena, hāyati maṇḍalena, hāyati ābhāya, hāyati ārohapariṇāhena.
root_text.sn16.7:3.2: Evameva kho, bhante, yassa kassaci saddhā natthi kusalesu dhammesu …pe…
root_text.sn16.7:3.6: paññā natthi … kusalesu dhammesu tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, hāniyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu, no vuddhi.
root_text.sn16.7:5.1: Yassa kassaci, bhante, saddhā atthi kusalesu dhammesu, hirī atthi kusalesu dhammesu, ottappaṁ atthi kusalesu dhammesu, vīriyaṁ atthi kusalesu dhammesu, paññā atthi kusalesu dhammesu, tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, vuddhiyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu, no parihāni.
root_text.sn16.7:6.1: Seyyathāpi, bhante, juṇhapakkhe candassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, vaḍḍhateva vaṇṇena, vaḍḍhati maṇḍalena, vaḍḍhati ābhāya, vaḍḍhati ārohapariṇāhena.
root_text.sn16.7:6.2: Evameva kho, bhante, yassa kassaci saddhā atthi kusalesu dhammesu …
root_text.sn16.7:6.6: paññā atthi kusalesu dhammesu tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, vuddhiyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu, no parihāni.
root_text.sn16.7:8.2: Yassa kassaci, kassapa, saddhā natthi kusalesu dhammesu …pe…
root_text.sn16.7:8.6: paññā natthi kusalesu dhammesu tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, hāniyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu, no vuddhi.
root_text.sn16.7:9.1: Seyyathāpi, kassapa, kāḷapakkhe candassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, hāyateva vaṇṇena …pe…
root_text.sn16.7:9.3: Evameva kho, kassapa, yassa kassaci saddhā natthi kusalesu dhammesu …pe…
root_text.sn16.7:9.7: paññā natthi kusalesu dhammesu tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, hāniyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu, no vuddhi.
root_text.sn16.7:10.1: Yassa kassaci, kassapa, saddhā atthi kusalesu dhammesu …pe…
root_text.sn16.7:10.5: paññā atthi kusalesu dhammesu tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, vuddhiyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu, no parihāni.
root_text.sn16.7:11.1: Seyyathāpi, kassapa, juṇhapakkhe candassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, vaḍḍhateva vaṇṇena, vaḍḍhati maṇḍalena, vaḍḍhati ābhāya, vaḍḍhati ārohapariṇāhena.
root_text.sn16.7:11.2: Evameva kho, kassapa, yassa kassaci saddhā atthi kusalesu dhammesu hirī atthi …
root_text.sn16.7:11.5: paññā atthi kusalesu dhammesu tassa yā ratti vā divaso vā āgacchati, vuddhiyeva pāṭikaṅkhā kusalesu dhammesu, no parihāni.

translation_text.sn16.7:1.1: Near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove.
translation_text.sn16.7:1.2: Then Venerable Mahākassapa went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side.
translation_text.sn16.7:1.6: Either you or I should advise the mendicants
translation_text.sn16.7:1.7: and give them a Dhamma talk.”
translation_text.sn16.7:2.1: “Sir, the mendicants these days are hard to admonish, having qualities that make them hard to admonish. They’re impatient, and don’t take instruction respectfully.
translation_text.sn16.7:2.2: Sir, whoever has no faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom when it comes to skillful qualities can expect decline, not growth, in skillful qualities, whether by day or by night.
translation_text.sn16.7:3.1: It’s like the moon in the waning fortnight. Whether by day or by night, its beauty, roundness, luminosity, and diameter and circumference only decline.
translation_text.sn16.7:3.2: In the same way, whoever has no faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom when it comes to skillful qualities can expect decline, not growth, in skillful qualities, whether by day or by night.
translation_text.sn16.7:4.1: A faithless individual is in decline.
translation_text.sn16.7:4.2: An individual with no conscience is in decline.
translation_text.sn16.7:4.3: An imprudent individual is in decline.
translation_text.sn16.7:4.4: A lazy individual is in decline.
translation_text.sn16.7:4.5: A witless individual is in decline.
translation_text.sn16.7:4.6: An irritable individual is in decline.
translation_text.sn16.7:4.7: An acrimonious individual is in decline.
translation_text.sn16.7:4.8: When there are no mendicant advisers there is decline.
translation_text.sn16.7:5.1: Sir, whoever has faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom when it comes to skillful qualities can expect growth, not decline, in skillful qualities, whether by day or by night.
translation_text.sn16.7:6.1: It’s like the moon in the waxing fortnight. Whether by day or by night, its beauty, roundness, luminosity, and diameter and circumference only grow.
translation_text.sn16.7:6.2: In the same way, whoever has faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom when it comes to skillful qualities can expect growth, not decline, in skillful qualities, whether by day or by night.
translation_text.sn16.7:7.2: An individual with a conscience doesn’t decline.
translation_text.sn16.7:7.8: When there are mendicant advisers there is no decline.”
translation_text.sn16.7:8.2: Whoever has no faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom when it comes to skillful qualities can expect decline, not growth …
translation_text.sn16.7:9.15: When there are no mendicant advisers there is decline.
translation_text.sn16.7:10.1: Whoever has faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom when it comes to skillful qualities can expect growth, not decline …
translation_text.sn16.7:12.8: When there are mendicant advisers there is no decline.”
